factors.summary: The company is deleveraging as it is in compliance with all covenants and believes it has sufficient liquidity.

evidence.text: The Company is in compliance with all covenants, including financial covenants, for all of its debt agreements.

factors.summary: Overall demand is decelerating, particularly in certain end markets.

evidence.text: The Company experienced lower sales in its Process businesses with exposure to oil and gas markets, in its Engineered Materials, Interconnects and Packaging businesses with metals markets exposure and in its highly competitive Floor Care and Specialty Motors businesses.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2016 compared with the second quarter of 2015

evidence.text: The Company's backlog of unfilled orders at June 30, 2016 was $1,138.0 million, a decrease of $9.8 million or 0.9%, compared with $1,147.8 million at December 31, 2015.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating, with internal sales declines.

evidence.text: The decrease in net sales for the second quarter of 2016 was due to a 7% internal sales decline, partially offset by a 4% increase from acquisitions.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2016 compared with the second quarter of 2015

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining, driven by weak global economic conditions.

evidence.text: The Company experienced lower sales in its Process businesses with exposure to oil and gas markets, in its Engineered Materials, Interconnects and Packaging businesses with metals markets exposure and in its highly competitive Floor Care and Specialty Motors businesses.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2016 compared with the second quarter of 2015

factors.summary: End markets such as oil and gas, metals, and floor care are slowing, while others are stable.

evidence.text: The Company experienced lower sales in its Process businesses with exposure to oil and gas markets, in its Engineered Materials, Interconnects and Packaging businesses with metals markets exposure and in its highly competitive Floor Care and Specialty Motors businesses.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2016 compared with the second quarter of 2015

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to lower net sales and increased costs.

evidence.text: Segment operating margins were 23.8% for the second quarter of 2016, compared with 25.2% for the second quarter of 2015. Cost of sales, excluding depreciation expense for the second quarter of 2016 was $621.6 million or 63.6% of net sales, a decrease of $12.3 million or 1.9%, compared with $633.9 million or 63.2% of net sales for the second quarter of 2015.

factors.summary: Restructuring and cost reduction programs have delivered savings but may not be sufficient to offset margin pressures.

evidence.text: The Company recorded realignment costs totaling $36.6 million, with $15.9 million recorded in the first quarter of 2015 and $20.7 million recorded in the fourth quarter of 2015 (the 2015 realignment costs).
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2016 compared with the second quarter of 2015

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, driven by reduced pension contributions and efficient operations.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities totaled $340.6 million for the first six months of 2016, an increase of $55.2 million or 19.3%, compared with $285.4 million for the first six months of 2015. Free cash flow (cash flow provided by operating activities less capital expenditures) was $315.3 million for the first six months of 2016, compared with $259.4 million for the first six months of 2015.

factors.summary: The company is consistently repurchasing shares, indicating confidence in its stock. The repurchases have increased significantly from the first half of 2015 to the first half of 2016, reflecting stronger management confidence.

evidence.text: The company is repurchasing shares under a $350 million authorization, indicating confidence in its stock.

For the first six months of 2016, the Company repurchased approximately 2,824,000 shares of its common stock for $135.3 million, compared with $57.2 million used for repurchases of approximately 1,075,000 shares for the first six months of 2015.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated active M&A activity with multiple acquisitions, but these activities are accompanied by integration challenges that have mixed effects on performance.

evidence.text: In July 2016, the Company acquired HS Foils and Nu Instruments for approximately $65 million utilizing cash outside the United States.

Contributions from the acquisitions of Brookfield Engineering Laboratories (Brookfield) and ESP/SurgeX in January 2016, Surface Vision in July 2015 and Global Tubes in May 2015, as well as the Companys Operational Excellence initiatives, including the 2015 realignment actions, had a positive impact on the second quarter of 2016 and the first six months of 2016 results.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2016 compared with the second quarter of 2015
